findings.evidence: grep hit: Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/TensorProduct.lean:191 shows the call order; PR map_tmul at line 214 claims r⊗f(a) which fails for the supplied definition.
findings.fix: swap arguments: Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map f (BialgHom.id K K)
findings.issue: map defined as Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map (BialgHom.id K K) f implements identity on A factor, extension on K factor; map_tmul / map_includeRight / map_antipode therefore state the converse of the documented scalar-extension map.

findings.evidence: line 238 proof relies on the mis-ordered call; definition of map_comp appears at PR line 177.
findings.fix: supply the intended BialgHom.id arguments on the correct hom positions after fixing map.
findings.issue: map_comp calls Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map_comp (BialgHom.id K K) (BialgHom.id K K) g f; parameter order means the call composes identities on the wrong sides.

summary: baseChange.map implements the swapped Maps; statements claim left-factor id + right-factor f but the definition and all dependent lemmas use the opposite order, falsifying functoriality.
